title: Public–Private Partnerships to Mitigate Salmonella Risks in Food Processing
author: Sophian, Alfi
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Salmonella     remains     one     of     the     leading     causes     of     foodborne     illness     worldwide  ,   posing     serious     public     health     and     economic     burdens  .   Contamination     can     occur     at     multiple     points   in   the     food     chain  ,   especially     during     processing  .   Public  –  private     partnerships   (  PPPs  )   offer     a     collaborative   model   to     reduce     Salmonella     risks     through     shared     resources  ,   joint     research  ,   and     coordinated     response     systems  . By   leveraging     government     oversight     and     industry     innovation  ,   PPPs     can     enhance     surveillance  ,   strengthen     hygiene     protocols  ,   and     improve     traceability     systems  .   This     approach     encourages     compliance     with     food     safety     regulations     while     maintaining     competitiveness   in global   markets  .   Investing   in PPP-  driven     programs     will     help     minimize     outbreaks  ,   protect     consumer     trust  ,   and     foster     a     culture     of     continuous     improvement   in   food     safety     management     systems     across     the     processing     sector  .
